title: SEM — Asylgesuche in der Schweiz stellen (Applying for asylum in Switzerland)
topic: asylum application, first steps, legal status, federal asylum centre, refugee process, Asylgesuch, Asylverfahren, Asylantrag, Flüchtling, Asylbewerber, Bundesasylzentrum
content: Official SEM overview for people applying for asylum in Switzerland (Asylgesuch stellen). Explains who can apply (wer kann ein Asylgesuch stellen), where to submit, who decides the case (Entscheid), and what happens after a decision. Asylum seekers (Asylsuchende) are assigned to a federal asylum centre (Bundesasylzentrum, BAZ) first.

title: SEM — Asylverfahren (Asylum procedure)
topic: asylum procedure, timeline, interview, refugee status, asylum decision, accelerated procedure, extended procedure, Asylverfahren, beschleunigtes Verfahren, erweitertes Verfahren, Anhörung, Asylentscheid
content: Official SEM page about the asylum procedure (Asylverfahren) in Switzerland. Two main procedures: the accelerated procedure (beschleunigtes Verfahren, decided within 140 days) and the extended procedure (erweitertes Verfahren, for complex cases assigned to a canton). Every applicant has the right to a hearing (Anhörung). After the hearing, SEM issues an asylum decision (Asylentscheid).

title: SEM — Wiederaufnahme syrischer Asylverfahren ab Mai 2026 (Syrian cases reopened from May 2026)
topic: Syria, Syrian asylum seekers, suspended files, case reopening, procedure resumption, Syrien, syrische Asylgesuche, sistierte Dossiers, Wiederaufnahme Verfahren, Assad, Syrer, pending cases Syria 2026, سوريا, سوريون, طالبو لجوء سوريون, ملفات معلقة, استئناف الإجراءات
content: SEM announced that suspended asylum files for Syrian nationals (syrische Asylgesuche / ملفات اللجوء السورية) will be reopened and processed from May 1, 2026. Following the fall of the Assad regime in December 2024, SEM had suspended (sistiert / علّق) processing of Syrian asylum applications to reassess the situation in Syria. From May 2026, SEM resumes examination (Wiederaufnahme des Verfahrens / استئناف النظر في الملفات) of all pending Syrian asylum cases. Syrians whose cases were on hold will be notified by SEM and their applications will be processed. Syrian asylum seekers with Permit N who have been waiting should expect their case to be reviewed. People with Permit F who are Syrian may also be affected. Contact SEM or your legal representative for updates.

title: SEM — Bundesasylzentren und Asylregionen (Asylum regions and federal asylum centres)
topic: federal asylum centre, asylum regions, accommodation, registration, canton, BAZ, Bundesasylzentrum, Asylregion, Unterkunft, Zuweisung, Kanton
content: Official SEM information about asylum regions and federal asylum centres (Bundesasylzentren, BAZ) in Switzerland. Asylum seekers first stay in a federal asylum centre (Bundesasylzentrum) where their application is registered and processed. After the federal phase (Bundesphase), those in the extended procedure (erweitertes Verfahren) are assigned to a canton (Zuweisung an einen Kanton).

title: SEM — Ausweis N (Permit N — asylum seekers in procedure)
topic: permit N, asylum seeker, N permit, asylum procedure, work restrictions, sojourn, Ausweis N, Asylsuchende, Arbeitsverbot, Aufenthaltsstatus
content: Permit N (Ausweis N) is issued to people who have applied for asylum (Asylsuchende) and whose case is still being decided. It is a temporary sojourn document valid during the asylum procedure. People with Permit N are generally not allowed to work (Arbeitsverbot) during the first three months in Switzerland. After three months, cantonal authorities (Kantonsbehörden) may grant permission to work (Arbeitsbewilligung). Permit N does not guarantee permanent residence.

title: SEM — Ausweis F (Permit F — vorläufig aufgenommene Personen)
topic: permit F, provisional admission, temporary admission, work, canton, identity document, Ausweis F, vorläufige Aufnahme, vorläufig aufgenommen, Arbeit, Kanton, Verlängerung
content: Official SEM page about Permit F (Ausweis F) for provisionally admitted foreigners (vorläufig aufgenommene Ausländerinnen und Ausländer). Temporary admission (vorläufige Aufnahme) is used when removal (Wegweisung) from Switzerland is unlawful, unreasonable, or impossible. Permit F is generally issued for 12 months and extended annually by the canton. People with Permit F may work in Switzerland with cantonal authorisation. The permit does not serve as proof of identity.

title: SEM — Ausweis S (Permit S — Schutzbedürftige)
topic: permit S, protection status S, Ukraine, work, canton, reporting obligations, Ausweis S, Schutzbedürftige, Schutzstatus, Ukraine Krieg, Arbeit
content: Official SEM page about Permit S (Ausweis S) for people in need of protection (Schutzbedürftige). Permit S allows people fleeing a war or civil conflict to stay in Switzerland without going through the full asylum procedure. Holders may work in Switzerland with a notification (Meldung) to the cantonal authority. Permit S was activated for people fleeing the war in Ukraine from 2022 (Ukraine-Krieg).

title: SEM — B EU/EFTA permit (Resident foreign nationals)
topic: permit B, residence permit, resident foreign nationals, EU EFTA, work, living in Switzerland
content: Official SEM page about the B EU/EFTA residence permit. This permit is for foreign nationals who reside in Switzerland for a longer period. Recognised refugees are also issued a Permit B. For EU/EFTA nationals, the permit is generally valid for five years. Recognised refugees with Permit B have the right to work in Switzerland.

title: SEM — C EU/EFTA permit (Settled foreign nationals)
topic: permit C, settlement permit, permanent residence, settled foreign nationals, EU EFTA, long-term
content: Official SEM page about the C EU/EFTA settlement permit. This permit is usually granted after five or ten years of residence in Switzerland and gives the right to stay that is not limited in time. It is one of the strongest permit categories in Switzerland.

title: SEM — Erwerbstätigkeit im Asylbereich (Work rights for asylum seekers and refugees)
topic: work, employment, asylum, refugee, permit B work rights, permit F work rights, permit S work rights, permit N work rights, Arbeit, Erwerbstätigkeit, Arbeitsbewilligung, Arbeitsgenehmigung, Beschäftigung
content: Official SEM page about work rights (Erwerbstätigkeit) for people in the asylum system. Recognised refugees with Permit B (anerkannte Flüchtlinge) may work freely. People with Permit F (vorläufig aufgenommen) may work with cantonal authorisation (Arbeitsbewilligung des Kantons). People with Permit S may work with a notification (Meldung) to the canton. People with Permit N may not work during the first three months; after that, cantonal authorisation (Bewilligung) is required.

title: ch.ch — Permits for living in Switzerland
topic: permits, residence permit, living in Switzerland, foreign nationals, permit renewal, permit overview
content: Official Swiss government portal page with an overview of all permit types for living in Switzerland. Explains the differences between Permit N, F, B, C, and S, when they apply, and how they can be extended or changed.

title: SEM — Integration und Einbürgerung (Integration overview)
topic: integration, integration program, KIP, cantonal integration program, language, social integration, employment integration, Integration, Sprachkurs, Kantonales Integrationsprogramm, KIP, Sprachförderung, Berufsintegration
content: Official SEM overview of integration (Integration) in Switzerland. Integration includes language learning (Sprachförderung), vocational integration (Berufsintegration), and social integration. Each canton runs a Cantonal Integration Program (Kantonales Integrationsprogramm, KIP) funded jointly by the federal government and cantons. Refugees and provisionally admitted persons (Flüchtlinge und vorläufig aufgenommene Personen) are entitled to integration measures.

title: SEM — Integration von Flüchtlingen und vorläufig aufgenommenen Personen
topic: integration, refugee integration, permit F integration, permit B refugee, language course, vocational, job market, integration program, Integration Flüchtlinge, vorläufig aufgenommen, Sprachkurs, Berufsintegration, Arbeitsmarkt
content: Recognised refugees (anerkannte Flüchtlinge, Permit B) and temporarily admitted persons (vorläufig aufgenommene Personen, Permit F) have access to specific integration programs (Integrationsprogramme) in Switzerland. These include language courses (Sprachkurse), early childhood programs, vocational preparation (Berufsintegration), and employment integration (Arbeitsmarktintegration). Programs are coordinated at the cantonal level.

title: FIDE — Language framework for integration in Switzerland
topic: FIDE, language test, German, French, Italian, language learning, integration, language certificate, A1, A2, B1
content: FIDE is the Swiss federal framework for language skills used in integration. It covers German, French, and Italian. Language courses and the FIDE language certificate are part of the integration pathway in Switzerland. Many cantons require a language certificate for permit renewal or integration agreements.

title: ch.ch — Learning languages in Switzerland
topic: language courses, German, French, Italian, language learning, integration, language school, FIDE
content: Official Swiss portal page about language learning for foreign nationals. Language courses are available across Switzerland through cantonal integration programs and various NGOs. Learning the local language (German, French, or Italian depending on the region) is a key step in integration and is often supported financially for refugees and asylum seekers.

title: SEM — Sozialhilfe und Gesundheit für Asylsuchende (Healthcare and social assistance)
topic: healthcare, medical care, health, social assistance, asylum seeker, federal centre, canton, basic needs, emergency care, Sozialhilfe, Gesundheitsversorgung, Krankenversicherung, Arzt, Asylsuchende, Grundversorgung
content: Official SEM page about social assistance (Sozialhilfe) and healthcare (Gesundheitsversorgung) for asylum seekers. During the federal phase, SEM provides accommodation (Unterkunft), food (Verpflegung), and emergency healthcare. After assignment to a canton, the canton takes over. Asylum seekers receive basic healthcare coverage (Grundversicherung). The level of social assistance (Sozialhilfe) is lower than regular Swiss welfare (Fürsorge) and covers basic needs only.

title: ch.ch — Health insurance and healthcare in Switzerland
topic: health insurance, KVG, healthcare, medical, hospital, doctor, insurance, premium reduction
content: Official Swiss portal page about the health insurance system in Switzerland. Everyone living in Switzerland is legally required to have basic health insurance (KVG/LAMal). For refugees and asylum seekers, the canton usually arranges a collective insurance contract. Premium reductions (Prämienverbilligung) are available for people with low income.

title: SEM — Bildung für Kinder von Asylsuchenden (Education rights for children)
topic: education, school, children, asylum seeker children, right to education, canton, schooling, Schule, Bildung, Kinder, Schulpflicht, Recht auf Bildung, Asylkinder
content: Children of asylum seekers and refugees (Kinder von Asylsuchenden und Flüchtlingen) in Switzerland have the right to attend school (Schulpflicht, Recht auf Bildung). Cantons are responsible for ensuring school access as soon as possible after arrival. This applies to children with Permit N, F, S, and B. In federal asylum centres (Bundesasylzentren), educational support is also provided.

title: SEM — Familiennachzug (Family reunification)
topic: family reunification, family, spouse, children, permit B refugee, permit F, family members, join family, Familiennachzug, Familie, Ehepartner, Kinder, Zusammenführung, Angehörige
content: Official SEM page about family reunification (Familiennachzug) in Switzerland. Recognised refugees with Permit B (anerkannte Flüchtlinge) may apply for family reunification within three years of being granted refugee status. People with Permit F (vorläufig aufgenommen) have more limited rights — they may apply after three years of provisional admission only if self-supporting (erwerbstätig). People with Permit N generally cannot bring family members (Familienangehörige) during the asylum procedure.

title: SEM — Negative Asylentscheid und Rechtsmittel (Negative decision and appeal)
topic: negative decision, appeal, rejection, Dublin, legal remedy, Federal Administrative Court, deadline, legal aid, Ablehnung, Beschwerde, abgelehnt, negativer Entscheid, Beschwerdefrist, Bundesverwaltungsgericht, Rechtsvertretung
content: If an asylum application is rejected (abgelehnt / negativer Asylentscheid), the person has the right to appeal (Beschwerde einreichen). In the accelerated procedure, the appeal deadline (Beschwerdefrist) is usually 7 days. In the extended procedure it is 30 days. Appeals go to the Federal Administrative Court (Bundesverwaltungsgericht, BVGer). Legal representation (Rechtsvertretung) is provided during the procedure. Act immediately after receiving a negative decision.

title: Federal Administrative Court — Asylum appeals
topic: Federal Administrative Court, FAC, appeal, asylum appeal, negative decision, court, legal remedy
content: The Federal Administrative Court (FAC) is the court that handles appeals against negative asylum decisions in Switzerland. If an asylum application is rejected by SEM, the applicant can appeal to the FAC within the legal deadline. The court reviews whether the decision was legally correct.

title: OSAR — Swiss Refugee Council (legal aid and advice)
topic: legal aid, legal advice, OSAR, SFH, Swiss Refugee Council, rights, support, NGO, appeal help
content: The Swiss Refugee Council (OSAR/SFH) is the main NGO providing legal information and support for asylum seekers and refugees in Switzerland. They provide advice on rights, the asylum procedure, and appeals. They are an important resource when someone receives a negative decision or has questions about their legal situation.

title: SEM — Einbürgerung (Naturalization — Swiss citizenship)
topic: naturalization, citizenship, Swiss passport, Swiss citizen, integration, years of residence, ordinary naturalization, facilitated naturalization, Einbürgerung, Staatsbürgerschaft, Schweizer Pass, Schweizer Bürger, ordentliche Einbürgerung, erleichterte Einbürgerung
content: Official SEM page about naturalization (Einbürgerung) — becoming a Swiss citizen (Schweizer Staatsbürger). Ordinary naturalization (ordentliche Einbürgerung) requires at least 10 years of residence in Switzerland. Recognised refugees (anerkannte Flüchtlinge) may have access to facilitated naturalization (erleichterte Einbürgerung). Applicants must demonstrate integration, language skills (Sprachkenntnisse), and knowledge of Swiss life. The process involves federal, cantonal, and municipal steps.

title: SEM — asylum-info.ch app
topic: asylum info, app, languages, life in Switzerland, healthcare, asylum centre, mobile app
content: Official SEM page about the asylum-info.ch app. The app provides general information in multiple languages about the asylum procedure, federal asylum centres, life in Switzerland, and healthcare.

title: Canton Zurich — Migration Office (Migrationsamt)
topic: canton Zurich, ZH, migration office, residence, permits, asylum, forms, contact, Zurich, Zürich
content: Official Canton of Zurich migration office: Migrationsamt des Kantons Zürich. Address: Berninastrasse 45, 8090 Zürich. Handles residence permits, asylum administration, work authorisation, and cantonal integration for people in Zurich. German-speaking canton.

title: Canton Bern — Migration Office (Amt für Bevölkerungsdienste)

content: Official Canton of Bern migration office: Amt für Bevölkerungsdienste des Kantons Bern. Address: Ostermundigenstrasse 99B, 3006 Bern. Email: midi.info@be.ch. Handles residence permits, asylum administration, work authorisation, and integration in Bern. German-speaking canton.

title: Canton Geneva — Office cantonal de la population et des migrations (OCPM)
topic: canton Geneva, migration office, OCPM, residence, permits, asylum, Geneva, French
content: Official Canton of Geneva migration office: OCPM — Office cantonal de la population et des migrations. Address: Route de Chancy 88, 1213 Onex (Genève). Handles residence permits, population registration, work authorisation, asylum, and cantonal integration in Geneva. French-speaking canton.

title: Canton Vaud — Service de la population (SPOP / Division étrangers)
topic: canton Vaud, VD, SPOP, migration office, residence, permits, asylum, Vaud, Lausanne, French

Handles residence permits, asylum, work authorisation, and integration for people in Vaud including Lausanne. French-speaking canton.

title: ch.ch — Rights and integration of refugees
topic: integration, rights, refugee, integration program, language, work, social, Switzerland overview
content: Official Swiss portal overview of integration rights and support for refugees and foreign nationals. Covers language learning, work rights, social integration, and where to get support across Switzerland.

topic: canton Graubünden, GR, Grisons, migration office, residence, permits, asylum, Graubünden, trilingual
content: Official Canton of Graubünden migration office. Handles residence permits, asylum administration, and integration in canton Graubünden. Trilingual canton (German, Romansh, Italian). Contact the Amt für Migration und Zivilrecht for permit questions.

title: Canton Jura — Service de la migration (SEM Jura)
topic: canton Jura, JU, migration office, residence, permits, asylum, Jura, French
content: Official Canton of Jura migration service. Handles residence permits, asylum administration, and integration matters in canton Jura. French-speaking canton. Contact the Service de la migration for permit questions and local support.

content: Official Canton of Neuchâtel migration service. Handles residence permits, asylum administration, and integration in canton Neuchâtel. French-speaking canton. Contact the Service des migrations for permit questions and cantonal integration support.

title: Canton Zug — Migration Office (Migrationsamt)
topic: canton Zug, ZG, migration office, contact, residence, permits, asylum, Zug, cantonal integration
content: Official Canton of Zug migration office (Migrationsamt). Contact the Migrationsamt Zug directly via their official website for permit questions, appointment booking, and local integration programme information.

content: Official Canton of Obwalden foreign nationals office. Handles residence permits, asylum administration, and integration in canton Obwalden. German-speaking canton.

topic: canton Uri, UR, migration office, residence, permits, asylum, Uri, integration
content: Official Canton of Uri migration office. Handles residence permits, asylum administration, and integration in canton Uri. German-speaking canton.

content: Official Canton of Appenzell Ausserrhoden migration office. Handles residence permits, asylum administration, and integration in canton Appenzell Ausserrhoden. German-speaking canton.

title: Canton Appenzell Innerrhoden — Migration (Ausländerrecht)
topic: canton Appenzell Innerrhoden, AI, migration office, residence, permits, asylum, Appenzell, integration
content: Official Canton of Appenzell Innerrhoden migration information. Handles residence permits, asylum administration, and integration in canton Appenzell Innerrhoden. German-speaking canton.

title: Canton Zurich — German Language Courses for Refugees and Asylum Seekers (Deutschkurse)
topic: canton Zurich, ZH, language course, German course, Deutschkurs, fide, A1, A2, B1, language learning, refugee, asylum seeker, integration, Zürich, Zurich, Sprachkurs, Deutschkurse Zürich
content: Canton Zurich offers subsidised German language courses (Deutschkurse) for refugees, asylum seekers (Permit N, F, B), and provisionally admitted persons. Courses follow the fide framework (national language integration standard). The Fachstelle Integration des Kantons Zürich coordinates course offerings. The City of Zurich also provides subsidised courses and course vouchers through its integration office. To find a course near you: use the fide course finder at fide-info.ch and filter for Kanton Zürich, or contact the Fachstelle Integration Kanton Zürich directly.

title: Canton Zurich — Cantonal Integration Program KIP (Kantonales Integrationsprogramm Zürich)
topic: canton Zurich, ZH, integration program, KIP, Integrationsprogramm, employment integration, Arbeitsintegration, language, asylum, refugee, social participation, Zürich, Zurich, integration coaching
content: The Canton of Zurich Cantonal Integration Program (Kantonales Integrationsprogramm, KIP) is funded jointly by the canton and the federal government. It includes German language promotion (Sprachförderung), employment integration (Arbeitsintegration), early childhood education, civic orientation, and social participation programs. Refugees (Permit B) and provisionally admitted persons (Permit F) are eligible. The Fachstelle Integration Kanton Zürich coordinates all programs and advises on available offerings. Contact via the Sicherheitsdirektion des Kantons Zürich.

title: Canton Geneva — French Language Courses for Refugees (Cours de français Genève)
topic: canton Geneva, GE, language course, French course, cours de français, fide, integration, refugee, asylum seeker, Geneva, Genève, Genf, language learning, A1, A2, B1, cours langue Genève
content: The Canton of Geneva has an official search tool to find French language courses (cours de français) for foreigners, refugees, and asylum seekers. Courses can be filtered by level, schedule, neighbourhood, and childcare availability. The Bureau de l'intégration et de la citoyenneté (BIC) manages course placement under the Swiss Integration Agenda. Many courses are free or heavily subsidised for Permit N, F, S, and B holders. To find courses: use this official Geneva search tool or the fide course finder at fide-info.ch filtering for Canton Geneva.

title: Canton Geneva — Integration Programs for Refugees (Programme d'intégration cantonal PIC Genève)
topic: canton Geneva, GE, integration program, PIC, programme intégration cantonal, AIS, agenda intégration, employment, refugees, asylum seekers, Geneva, Genève, Réfugiés au travail, civic orientation
content: The Canton of Geneva runs the Programme d'intégration cantonal (PIC) under the national Swiss Integration Agenda (AIS). Programs include: language and integration courses, early language assessment (bilan de compétences linguistiques), employment insertion (Réfugiés@Work), summer French courses (Français en Été), and civic orientation (connaissance de la Suisse). The BIC (Bureau de l'intégration et de la citoyenneté) manages all programs. Permit N, F, S, and B holders are eligible. Contact the OCPM or BIC for access and enrolment.

title: Canton Bern — German Language Courses for Refugees (Deutschkurse Kanton Bern / DeutschBon)
topic: canton Bern, BE, language course, German course, Deutschkurs, fide, integration, refugee, asylum seeker, Bern, language learning, A1, A2, B1, DeutschBon, Deutschkurse Bern, Sprachförderung
content: Canton Bern offers subsidised German language courses for refugees, asylum seekers (Permit N, F), and provisionally admitted persons through the Amt für Integration und Soziales (AIS). The City of Bern runs the DeutschBon voucher program for approved language schools. Courses are available from A1 to B2 level; many schools offer childcare (Kinderbetreuung). To find courses: use fide-info.ch and filter for Kanton Bern, or contact the AIS office at Postgasse 68, 3011 Bern. Verify current voucher conditions at gsi.be.ch.

title: Canton Bern — Cantonal Integration Program KIP 3 (Kantonales Integrationsprogramm Bern)
topic: canton Bern, BE, integration program, KIP, KIP 3, Integrationsprogramm, employment, language, social integration, asylum, refugee, Bern, Amt für Integration, Arbeitsintegration, Sprachförderung Bern
content: The Cantonal Integration Program KIP 3 (2024–2027) of Canton Bern is managed by the Amt für Integration und Soziales (AIS). It includes German language support (Sprachförderung), employment integration (Arbeitsintegration), early childhood programs (Frühförderung), civic orientation (Orientierung in der Schweiz), and social participation. Refugees (Permit B) and provisionally admitted persons (Permit F) are priority beneficiaries. Contact: Amt für Integration und Soziales, Postgasse 68, 3011 Bern.

title: Canton Zug — German Language Courses for Refugees (Subventionierte Deutschkurse Kanton Zug)
topic: canton Zug, ZG, language course, German course, Deutschkurs, fide, integration, refugee, asylum seeker, Zug, language learning, A1, A2, B1, subventionierte Deutschkurse
content: Canton Zug subsidises German language courses (A1–B2 level) through approved providers including Pro Arbeit, SAH Zentralschweiz, and SRK (Swiss Red Cross). fide-certified instruction is available. Courses are aimed at migrants with low incomes, including asylum seekers and refugees (Permit N, F, B). Personalised counselling is available through the Fachstelle Migration Zug. Contact: integration@zg.ch, Neugasse 2, 6301 Zug. Also search fide-info.ch for courses in Kanton Zug.

title: Canton Zug — Cantonal Integration Program KIP (Kantonales Integrationsprogramm Zug)
topic: canton Zug, ZG, integration program, KIP, Integrationsprogramm, employment, language, social integration, asylum, refugee, Zug, civic orientation, Fachstelle Migration
content: The Canton of Zug Cantonal Integration Program (KIP) covers first information, language promotion, early childhood, and social participation. The Fachstelle Migration Zug (Migration Specialist Office) offers daily-life counselling and language courses for asylum seekers and refugees. Contact: integration@zg.ch, Neugasse 2, 6301 Zug. Refugees (Permit B) and provisionally admitted persons (Permit F) are eligible for KIP programs.

title: Canton Aargau — German Language Courses for Refugees (Sprachförderung Kanton Aargau)
topic: canton Aargau, AG, language course, German course, Deutschkurs, Sprachförderung, fide, integration, refugee, asylum seeker, Aargau, Aarau, Baden, language learning, A1, A2, B1, Alphabetisierung
content: Canton Aargau subsidises German language courses from literacy/alphabetisation level through B1, offered in Aarau, Baden, and six regional locations — many with childcare. Programs include intensive A1 Plus courses and courses specifically for women. All courses are fide-certified. Permit N, F, and B holders are eligible for subsidised rates. Contact: integration@ag.ch. Additional course information at integrationaargau.ch and hallo-aargau.ch. Also use the fide course finder at fide-info.ch for Kanton Aargau.

title: Canton Aargau — Cantonal Integration Program KIP (Kantonales Integrationsprogramm Aargau)
topic: canton Aargau, AG, integration program, KIP, KIP 3, Integrationsprogramm, employment, language, social integration, asylum, refugee, Aargau, Regional Integration Centers, RIF, Arbeitsintegration
content: The Aargau Cantonal Integration Program (KIP 3, 2024–2027) combines integration measures with employment and education mainstreaming. The canton has organised 60+ municipalities into Regional Integration Centers (Regionale Integrationsfachstellen, RIF) that coordinate local programs. Financing is shared equally by the federal government, canton, and municipalities. Contact: Integration Section, Migrationsamt Aargau, Bahnhofstrasse 88, 5001 Aarau, integration@ag.ch.

title: Canton Schaffhausen — Language Courses and Asylum Support (Asyl- und Flüchtlingsbetreuung SH)
topic: canton Schaffhausen, SH, language course, German course, Deutschkurs, asylum, refugee, integration, Schaffhausen, asylum support, Flüchtlingsbetreuung, Migrationsamt Schaffhausen
content: In Canton Schaffhausen, asylum and refugee care (Asyl- und Flüchtlingsbetreuung) is coordinated by the Sozialamt. Language courses and integration support for asylum seekers and refugees are managed in cooperation with the Migrationsamt Schaffhausen. For specific language course offerings and enrolment, contact the Migrationsamt Schaffhausen or the cantonal Sozialamt directly. Also use fide-info.ch to find courses in Kanton Schaffhausen.

title: Canton Schaffhausen — Cantonal Integration Program KIP (Integrationsförderung Schaffhausen)
topic: canton Schaffhausen, SH, integration program, KIP, KIP 3, Integrationsprogramm, Integrationsförderung, employment, language, asylum, refugee, Schaffhausen, social participation
content: Canton Schaffhausen implements the Cantonal Integration Program (KIP 3, 2024–2027) through the Sozialamt and Migrationsamt. Integration promotion (Integrationsförderung) covers language, employment, early childhood, and social participation. In small cantons like Schaffhausen, integration programs and language courses are often coordinated directly by the migration office. Contact the Migrationsamt Schaffhausen at migrationsamt.sh.ch for integration programme details and enrolment.

title: Canton Lucerne — German Language Courses for Refugees (Deutsch lernen DAF Kanton Luzern)
topic: canton Lucerne, LU, language course, German course, Deutschkurs, fide, integration, refugee, asylum seeker, Lucerne, Luzern, language learning, A1, A2, DAF, mandatory German courses
content: The Canton of Lucerne's Department for Asylum and Refugees (DAF) offers mandatory German language courses (Deutschkurse) three times per week for asylum seekers and provisionally admitted persons. Courses cover literacy through A2 level and are fide-certified. Instruction focuses on everyday practical language use. Contact: Abteilung Kurswesen, Museggstrasse 37, 6004 Luzern. Email: kurswesen.daf@lu.ch. Also use fide-info.ch for additional courses in Kanton Luzern.

title: Canton Lucerne — Cantonal Integration Program KIP 3 (Kantonales Integrationsprogramm Luzern)
topic: canton Lucerne, LU, integration program, KIP, KIP 3, Integrationsprogramm, employment, language, asylum, refugee, Lucerne, Luzern, DISG, social participation, Arbeitsintegration
content: The Cantonal Integration Program KIP 3 (2024–2027) of Canton Lucerne covers seven promotion areas including language, employment, early childhood, and social participation. The program supports both migrants arriving via family reunion/employment and recognised refugees/provisionally admitted persons (Permit B, F). Managed by the Departement Gesundheit und Soziales (DISG), Rösslimattstrasse 37, Lucerne. Contact the DISG integration team for enrolment and programme access.

title: Canton Basel-Stadt — German Language Courses for Refugees (Deutsch lernen Basel-Stadt)
topic: canton Basel-Stadt, BS, Basel, language course, German course, Deutschkurs, fide, integration, refugee, asylum seeker, Basel, language learning, A1, A2, B1, Deutschkursgutscheine, voucher
content: Canton Basel-Stadt offers comprehensive German learning support for refugees and asylum seekers. Residents with Permit B receive 80 free lesson vouchers (Deutschkursgutscheine) upon arrival, valid for one year. Reduced course fees are available for all canton residents. Childcare for children under 5 is co-financed. Free courses are also available for parents at selected primary schools. For Permit S holders (Ukrainians), contact Social Services at Klybeckstrasse 15, Basel. Full provider directory at deutschkurse.bs.ch. Also search fide-info.ch for Basel courses.

title: Canton Basel-Stadt — Cantonal Integration Program KIP 3 (Fachstelle Integration Basel-Stadt)
topic: canton Basel-Stadt, BS, Basel, integration program, KIP, KIP 3, Integrationsprogramm, Fachstelle Integration, employment, language, asylum, refugee, Basel, civic orientation, anti-discrimination
content: Basel-Stadt's Cantonal Integration Program KIP 3 (2024–2027) focuses on seven areas: information and counselling, language, employment, early childhood, social participation, diversity and anti-discrimination, and interpretation services. Managed by the Fachstelle Integration und Antirassismus. Address: Schneidergasse 7, 4051 Basel. Email: integration@bs.ch. Refugees (Permit B) and provisionally admitted persons (Permit F) are priority beneficiaries.

title: Canton Vaud — Language Courses and Integration Support for Refugees (CSIR Vaud)
topic: canton Vaud, VD, language course, French course, cours de français, fide, integration, refugee, asylum seeker, Vaud, Lausanne, CSIR, language learning, A1, A2, informatique, computer skills
content: The Centre social d'intégration des réfugiés (CSIR) in Vaud serves recognised refugees and provisionally admitted persons (Permit B and F). Programs include French language courses, mathematics, computer skills, office software, and life integration workshops. CSIR Lausanne: Route des Plaines-du-Loup 1, 1014 Lausanne. Additional branches in Montreux and Yverdon serve other parts of the canton. Also use fide-info.ch to find French courses in Canton Vaud.

title: Canton Vaud — Cantonal Integration Program PIC 3 (Bureau cantonal pour l'intégration BCI Vaud)
topic: canton Vaud, VD, integration program, PIC, PIC 3, programme intégration cantonal, BCI, AIS, employment, language, asylum, refugee, Vaud, Lausanne, civic orientation, insertion professionnelle
content: The Bureau cantonal pour l'intégration des étrangers et la prévention du racisme (BCI) coordinates the Vaud Cantonal Integration Program (PIC III, 2024–2027), implementing around 100 measures across seven integration domains. Main office: Avenue de Sévelin 46, 1014 Lausanne. Email: info.integration@vd.ch. Three regional branches cover the entire canton. BCI provides counselling to migrants and professionals, and coordinates employment insertion and language support programs.

title: Canton Obwalden — German Language Courses for Refugees (Deutschkurse Kanton Obwalden)
topic: canton Obwalden, OW, language course, German course, Deutschkurs, fide, integration, refugee, asylum seeker, Obwalden, Sarnen, language learning, FrauKi, MuKi, Alpha, literacy, Sprachkurs
content: Canton Obwalden subsidises German language courses at up to 70% cost coverage for refugees and asylum seekers. Courses are offered through the Bildungszentrum (BWZ) in Sarnen and Engelberg. Specialised offerings include: FrauKi-Deutschkurs (Thursday afternoons with childcare, CHF 3 per lesson), MuKi courses (Tuesday and Thursday mornings), Alpha literacy courses, and Plaudertasche (children aged 1.5–5 years). Contact the Fachstelle Gesellschaftsfragen Obwalden via ow.ch. Also use fide-info.ch for courses in Kanton Obwalden.

title: Canton Obwalden — Cantonal Integration Program KIP 3 (Kantonales Integrationsprogramm Obwalden)
topic: canton Obwalden, OW, integration program, KIP, KIP 3, Integrationsprogramm, employment, language, asylum, refugee, Obwalden, Sarnen, civic orientation, integration counselling
content: Obwalden's KIP 3 (2024–2027) supports projects promoting coexistence and integration of migrants. Free and confidential integration counselling is available for all migrants on integration topics. Contact: Fachstelle Gesellschaftsfragen Obwalden. Email: integration@ow.ch. The Fachstelle coordinates all integration programs in the canton.
